alex is a technical support specialist who spends a significant amount of time troubleshooting issues with google drive. every day, alex handles an average of 20 support tickets. of these, 40% are related to file access issues, 35% are related to data security concerns, and the remaining are related to miscellaneous issues. on a particular day, alex manages to resolve all of the file access and data security tickets, but only half of the miscellaneous issues. 

the total number of support tickets alex resolved on that day can be calculated as follows: 40% of 20 is 8, 35% of 20 is 7, and the remaining 25% is 5. the miscellaneous issues were resolved half of 5, which is 2.5. adding these together, alex resolved 8 + 7 + 2.5 = 17.5 support tickets on that day. 

the number of support tickets alex resolved on that day is 17.5, which is not a whole number. this suggests that the initial assumptions about the distribution of ticket types may need to be re-evaluated.
